body.body:
Surfaced during T-goal-graph-use-cortex-store sprint 2026-04-15. grep of tools/ for 'INSERT INTO memories' found multiple bypass sites. Most are seed_*.py scripts (arguably legitimate for genesis/boot paths) but two are runtime tools that should be converted:

## Confirmed bypasses

- habit_chunker.py:121 — INSERT INTO memories in a runtime habit-chunking path
- skill_importer.py:258 — INSERT INTO memories (id, memory_type, narrative, metadata, payload, timestamp) for skill import
- memory_sync.py:68, 204 — explicitly the sync layer, may be legitimate
- seed_*.py (many) — seed_cc_direction_habit, seed_tool_discovery, seed_inhibit_mid_conv_greeting, seed_strategic_goals, seed_reply_obligation_look, etc.

## Why this matters

cortex.store() is the single place where per-memory metadata stamps apply: scrub, credential leakage filter, test_data tagging (IGOR_TEST_MODE), D256 id regeneration, link auto-creation. Every bypass is a hole in those systems. The test-pollution bug this morning was caused exactly by one of these bypasses (goal_graph) — the tag never reached the table so session cleanup couldn't find it.

## Scope

1. Convert habit_chunker and skill_importer to use cortex.store() with proper Memory objects (same pattern as goal_graph fix, commit 362f969e)
2. Decide on seed_*.py scripts: either convert them OR document the exemption (seed scripts bypass scrub+test-data on purpose because they run at boot with known-clean genesis content). My recommendation: document the exemption and add a marker comment "# GENESIS BYPASS — do not run at runtime" on each seed file
3. memory_sync.py is the sync layer, should stay as-is but document why
4. Add an audit check to audit_checks.json: grep for 'INSERT INTO memories' in tools/ excluding seed_*.py and memory_sync.py; fail if any new bypass appears

## Out of scope

- Converting every seed_*.py (too many, low value)
- Refactoring memory_sync (it's the layer that does sync, not a consumer)

## Provenance

Grep during T-goal-graph-use-cortex-store audit. The goal_graph bypass caused today's test-pollution feedback loop; this ticket prevents the next instance.
body.title: Audit runtime tools/ for cortex.store() bypasses — single-chokepoint enforcement
body.result: Converted 6 runtime tools (habit_chunker, skill_importer, after_action_reviewer, output_trainer, learner, self_trainer) to cortex.store. Audit check audit_check_cortex_bypass.py registered as no-cortex-store-bypass. Exempts seed_*.py + memory_sync.py. Zero regressions, 2520 total pass.
